explanation: The natural language requirement states that the system must provide a log to include results of the pre-use check. The atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck' (indicating 'log results of the pre-use check') directly matches this requirement. The best LTL property is to state that 'eventually, the system will log the results of the pre-use check', which can be represented using the 'finally' (F) operator. This ensures that at some point during system operation, 'logPreUseCheck' will become true, satisfying the requirement that the system shall provide this log. A reasonable duration N_DURATION=3 is selected to reflect a bounded temporal expectation for logging; this can be adjusted as needed.

The requirement states that the system shall provide a log to include results of the pre-use check. There is an atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck' defined as 'indicates log results of the pre-use check'. Therefore, the requirement is that eventually, the log of pre-use check results must happen. To capture this, we use the LTL template for eventuality ('F bool_exp3') and set bool_exp3 as 'logPreUseCheck'. N_DURATION can be a reasonable finite interval—for this, we choose N_DURATION = 10 as a concrete value, but the key is to use a finite duration. This property expresses that, within 10 time steps, logging of pre-use check results must occur.

The requirement states that the system must provide a log which includes the results of the pre-use check. In the atomic propositions, 'logPreUseCheck' directly indicates 'log results of the pre-use check.' Thus, the property should state that it is always the case (globally) that the pre-use check results are logged—i.e., 'logPreUseCheck' must happen. The LTL template 'G bool_exp3' expresses that 'bool_exp3' is always true throughout the execution. By setting 'bool_exp3' to 'logPreUseCheck', we directly formalize the requirement that those log entries exist throughout the system's operation.

The requirement is that the system must create a log that includes the results of the pre-use check. The atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck' directly indicates logging of pre-use check results. Thus, the temporal property 'F logPreUseCheck' (eventually logPreUseCheck) captures the requirement that, at some point in the system execution, the log of the pre-use check must be produced. Chosen template 9 ('F bool_exp3') is appropriate here, as it expresses an eventual requirement.

The input requirement states that the system must provide a log that includes results of the pre-use check. The atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck' specifically indicates logging the results of the pre-use check. Therefore, the LTL property should require that, always (globally), the system eventually (at some point) logs the results of the pre-use check. The best fitting template is 'F bool_exp3', which translates to 'eventually, logPreUseCheck must be true'. This matches the intent of ensuring the system provides a log for the pre-use check results at some point in its execution.

The requirement states that the system must provide a log that includes the results of the pre-use check. In terms of the available atomic propositions, 'logPreUseCheck' denotes that the system logs the results of the pre-use check. Thus, the requirement translates directly to stating that (globally) it will always eventually happen that the log of the pre-use check occurs. The best matching LTL template is the 'eventually' template, indicating that the desired event (logPreUseCheck) must occur at some point in the execution (ideally after the pre-use check is done, but since no explicit ordering is stated in the requirement, we simply require that it happens).

The requirement states that the system shall provide a log to include the results of the pre-use check. Among the atomic propositions, 'logPreUseCheck' directly indicates the logging of the results of the pre-use check. Therefore, the property needed is that at some future time point, the system ensures this logging takes place. Template 9 (F bool_exp3) best fits, expressing that eventually the results of the pre-use check are logged (i.e., 'logPreUseCheck' becomes true).

The natural language requirement specifies that the system must provide a log containing results of the pre-use check. Mapping to the atomic propositions, 'logPreUseCheck' directly represents logging the results of the pre-use check. A straightforward mapping to an LTL property is to require that, globally, the system should eventually provide (i.e., trigger) this logging action. Thus, we specify that 'eventually' (F) 'logPreUseCheck' will be true at some point, ensuring compliance with the requirement.

The requirement specifies that the system must provide a log including the results of the pre-use check. The atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck' directly represents 'log results of the pre-use check'. Therefore, the global property G(logPreUseCheck) ensures that it is always the case (throughout operation) that the action of logging pre-use check results occurs, satisfying the requirement that such a log is provided and included.

The natural language states that the system must provide a log that includes results of the pre-use check. This means that whenever the pre-use check has been completed (preUseCheckDone), a log with the results (logPreUseCheck) should be provided. This requirement is best captured as an "everywhere, if A then eventually B" pattern. Therefore, we use template 14: G (A -> F[0,N_DURATION] B), where A is preUseCheckDone and B is logPreUseCheck. This ensures that every time a pre-use check is done, the system will (within N_DURATION steps) provide a log of its results, as required.
